entries.title: pipeline-orchestrator
entries.description: v8.22.0 (2026-07-07, MINOR — hook-unblock pack: 5 anti-deadlock/anti-friction fixes to the enforcement layer, from the 2026-07-07 corrective-telemetry diagnosis (27 triggers; live egg-and-chicken deadlock killed run 2026-07-04-e2e-ruler-recalibration-s2 with 0/320 diff): (T1) ALREADY-DISPATCHED exemption — pendingAlreadyDispatched + dispatchTargetMatches in edit-guard-hook.cjs treat a DISPATCH_REQUEST as satisfied once the target subagent was genuinely spawned (pending_dispatches record from dispatch-record-hook, created_at >= emitted_at, leaf-containment match mirroring isResolutionAgent) — frees all 3 session blockers at once; block between REQUEST and spawn, tamper fail-closed and malformed-shape guard preserved. (T2) ANCHORED exec-window control-plane exemption in dispatch-pending-gate.cjs — a Bash/PowerShell command that IS exactly node …/scripts/exec-window/open.cjs|close.cjs (env-var prefixes tolerated) passes with a live pending; composition (&&, ;, |, backtick, newline, subshell) or the path embedded as another program argument never does (anti-injection pinned by tests). (T3) DETERMINISTIC pending clear — step-ledger-stamp.cjs (PostToolUse:Agent) clearSatisfiedPending removes the returning agent-matching DISPATCH_REQUEST entry from the signed state (withLock + read-verify-refuse-launder, record-step posture), replacing the honor-only controller prose whose omission caused a false 482-min PROTOCOL_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT. (T4) DISK-TRUTH anti-false-timeout — pendingResolvedByEvents: a pending whose matching DISPATCH_RESULT is already recorded in the run protocol-events.jsonl (timestamp >= request) is RESOLVED, wired into findLivePendingBlock AND the cleanup-orphan checkHandshakeTimeouts sweep (implementing its own docstring promise "AND no matching response arrived"); DISPATCH-only scope (GATE/PLAN_MODE stay human-resolved). (T5) STAMP-BY-EVIDENCE — step-ledger-gate.cjs grants the tdd step when gate-decisions.jsonl carries TDD_APPROVAL APPROVED|CONFIRMED (frozen RED tests from a prior session), persists the stamp via recordStep and leaves a STEP_STAMP_BY_EVIDENCE audit event in protocol-events (NOT a new gate — Iron Law preserved: Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED). Tests tests/regression/v8.22.0/F1-F5 (41 scenarios, TDD RED->GREEN per task); suite 258/8 = pre-existing baseline exactly (3x Langfuse + score-writer + F4 v8.8.0 + 3x v8.21.0 frozen RED from the in-flight S2 slice), 0 new failures. Authored via the loopify harness (loop/hook-unblock branch, per-task selective commits); headless loop hit the harness sensitive-file wall on .claude/hooks/** — implementation landed from the interactive session, lesson recorded in loops/hook-unblock/LOOP_DONE.md. Anterior: v8.20.0 (2026-07-02, MINOR — enforcement unblocking pack from the 2026-07-02 hook audit): (1) STOP-TRAP FAIL-OPEN: the three Stop-family gates (stop-gate-hook, e2e-eval-gate, subagent-stop-commit-hook) blocked FOREVER when their continuity counter could not be persisted (signer unavailable / verifyState failing at write time / read-only state or dir) — persist()/withSignedState() now report real commit success and each block site fails OPEN with an audit trace, extending the existing !statePath B5 guard to every unpersistable shape. (2) scope-lock-hook emits the canonical hookSpecificOutput PreToolUse envelope — the old FLAT top-level shape was not honored by the harness, so the refactor scope deny was either ignored or bound without its reason ever reaching the model. (3) PIPELINE_NOT_ARMED / CORRUPT_STATE denies + the arm-writer/session-arm banners rewritten to ONE reachable next action (dispatch Agent(subagent_type: pipeline-orchestrator:core:pipeline-controller)) — the impossible write-sentinel-state-by-hand recipe (which contradicted MARKER_TAMPERED and deepened the block) and the literal {PIPELINE_DOC_PATH}/<cwd> placeholders are GONE. (4) lib/pipeline-arm.cjs HARNESS_OPEN_TAG now also cuts teammate-message/agent-message envelopes — a background subagent report QUOTING /pipeline-orchestrator:pipeline continue in prose armed a phantom CONTINUE run live on 2026-07-02, bricking the session Bash/PowerShell/Agent until the TTL. (5) NEW .claude/hooks/sentinel-state-autosign.cjs (PostToolUse Write|Edit|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit): signs the Bash-less controller freshly-written UNSIGNED run-dir sentinel-state.json (NEVER touches a present signature — invalid = tamper evidence; SKIPS under PIPELINE_HMAC_STRICT; fail-open) — closes STATE_FILE_INIT_FAIL and most corrective-loop noise; zero security delta (unsigned was already tolerated by default). TDD tests/regression/v8.20.0/F1-F5 (26 scenarios RED-to-GREEN incl. a live-repro of the phantom arm). Iron Law: only .claude/hooks/ + lib/ + hooks/hooks.json + tests/; Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 257/5 (same 5 pre-existing). Lockstep all version surfaces. v8.19.3 (2026-07-01, PATCH — arm-classifier: keyword-inferred read-only runs no longer arm [recurring phantom-arm deadlock fix]): a natural-language review/audit/analysis request ('faça revisão adversarial', 'faça uma auditoria', 'análise') was classified FULL/Audit and armed the pipeline-arm-pending marker — but read-only work never opens an exec window, so it hit INLINE_WORK_BLOCKED, aborted, and left an orphan marker that bricked the next task with PIPELINE_NOT_ARMED (observed live in interview-coach 2026-06-30 and 07-01). FIX: lib/pipeline-arm.cjs::writeArmPending now also skips arming for the read-only TYPES (Audit, UX Simulation) when they were INFERRED FROM KEYWORDS (source kw:), while an EXPLICIT --audit/--ux flag stays a deliberate governed run and still arms (preserving the STEP_1_7 direct-entry evidence, v8.16.0). Safe direction per the documented arm trade-off: a missed arm is recoverable (re-invoke); a phantom arm deadlocks. TDD tests/regression/v8.19.3/F1-readonly-types-no-arm (RED 4-fail → GREEN); 2 deliberate v8.7.0 pins updated to the --audit-flag path. Iron Law: only lib/pipeline-arm.cjs + tests/ touched; Gates/Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 252/5 (5 pre-existing Langfuse/env/F4). Lockstep across all version surfaces. v8.19.2 (2026-07-01, PATCH — edit-guard-hook robustness [Cursor-crash hardening, Claude-Code-scoped]): the PreToolUse edit-guard no longer crashes into the opaque external catch ('edit-guard-hook internal error — failing closed') when .pipeline/sessions is present-but-unreadable (a file where a dir is expected → ENOTDIR/EACCES) — getActiveLock + getActiveExecWindow now guard readdirSync and return null instead of throwing, and the CLI external catch surfaces the REAL sanitized error (single-line, printable-ASCII, capped 200) instead of the opaque message, so a genuine failure stays fail-closed but debuggable. Posture UNCHANGED (still deny when the guard cannot evaluate); NO foreign tool-name vocabulary added — cross-runtime tool mapping is the other editor's concern, deliberately out of scope. TDD tests/regression/v8.19.2/F1 (RED 7-fail → GREEN, 8 checks). Iron Law: only .claude/hooks/edit-guard-hook.cjs + tests/ touched; Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 251/5 (5 pre-existing Langfuse/env/F4). Lockstep 9 surfaces. v8.19.1 (2026-06-30, PATCH — arm-marker consume-on-close deadlock fix): the pipeline-arm-pending marker (written at /pipeline invocation, 30-min TTL) was never consumed when a governed run ended deliberately, so a finished code-mutating run's still-live marker kept blocking the NEXT non-pipeline task in the same session with PIPELINE_NOT_ARMED until TTL/restart (REVIEW-ONLY/DIAGNOSTIC never arm — the trigger is a FULL/code run that armed, completed, and was never disarmed). FIX: stop-gate-hook.cjs consumes the marker on its closed-run ALLOW paths (completed/GO-completer/spec-seal/hard_failed-cap/deliberate-inactive) via a new session-aware lib/pipeline-arm.cjs::clearArmPendingForSession — never on a block, on SessionEnd/StopFailure recovery, or on corrupt state. Positive-ownership delete (deletes only when marker+caller session_ids are both present and equal, OR both absent; ambiguous/mismatched preserved for TTL) + tamper verdict delegated to the lib/arm-pending SSOT (a signed-but-invalid marker stays fail-closed). 2 zero-context adversarial rounds (1 HIGH tamper-bypass + 1 CRITICAL cross-session-delete, both fixed) then a final pass CLEAN. TDD tests/regression/v8.19.1/F1 (10 scenarios, all 5 call sites + tamper + ownership). Iron Law: only .claude/hooks/stop-gate-hook.cjs + lib/pipeline-arm.cjs + tests/ touched; Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 250/5 (5 pre-existing). Lockstep 9 surfaces. v8.17.0 (2026-06-28, MINOR — autonomous bugfix run delivers 4 audit-driven fixes + 1 meta-escape) — (AUDIT-001 MED-LOW) `.claude/hooks/stop-hook.cjs` adds tail-read verify after `appendRunLog`: on mismatch, a single `run-log verify mismatch` warn fires on stderr; soft-fail on missing/malformed; exported as `verifyRunLogAppend` for unit testing. (AUDIT-004 CRIT, deployment) new `lib/enforcement-status.cjs::detectEnforcementStatus({hooksDir, expectedCanonicalHook})` probes the install for the canonical execution-gate hook (`pipeline-arm-gate.cjs`); wired into the existing SessionStart `enforcement-surface-verify-hook.cjs` to emit a dedicated `ENFORCEMENT_INACTIVE` protocol-event with HIGH severity when missing — registered in `lib/contracts/e2e-eval.cjs::HYGIENE_EVENT_SEVERITY` at weight 1.0 so an install with the gate missing cannot reach clean ProcessHygiene. (AUDIT-005 HIGH) `lib/audit-read-budget.cjs::buildAuditCorrectiveDescriptor` adds closed-vocabulary machine-readable fields `corrective_action:"dispatch_agent"` + `agent_type:"audit-intake"`; `buildAuditInlineReason` appends a `[corrective_action=dispatch_agent agent_type=audit-intake]` suffix to the prose reason so a parent reading only text can still pattern-match the recovery action. (AUDIT-006 NEW MED) `lib/e2e-evaluator.cjs::scoreRun` short-circuits on aborted runs (`state.abort_reason` non-empty + `pipeline_outcome` starting with `ABORTED_`): drops StepCompletion / GateCoverage / OrderIntegrity (renormalized over ProcessHygiene), emits exactly one AUDIT-class `hygiene_violation` named `PIPELINE_ABORTED` severity=high weight=1.0 — so aborted runs surface as aborts instead of being scored as catastrophic failures. (META — user-authorized chain-tied deadlock escape) `.claude/hooks/skill-frontmatter-parser.cjs::getEnforcementMode` honours the `PIPELINE_AUTONOMOUS_RECOVERY_MODE=true` env flag by DOWNGRADING `deny` to `warn` for the process (never escalates `warn`; case-sensitive `"true"` only; one stderr audit line per process). The flag institutionalizes the user-authorized self-recovery path: when a parent must dispatch multiple agents in sequence under autonomous /goal full autonomy, the chain-tied enforcement converts to WARN so the run can complete instead of deadlocking at the gate. TDD RED→GREEN via tests/regression/v8.17.0/F1-F5 (5 files, 35+ cases); full suite 4 fail (the 4 pre-existing Langfuse/env baseline — F8/F9/F10 langfuse + F2 score-writer — unchanged). Iron Law preserved: only `.claude/hooks/`, `.codex/hooks/`, `lib/` and `tests/` touched; the 35-row Mandatory Gates table + 49-row Gate Registry UNTOUCHED. Lockstep 9 surfaces. v8.16.0 (2026-06-28, MINOR — autonomous bugfix of 2 glitches caught by the v8.15.0 E2E evaluator during the v8.15.1 self-audit run, both deterministic-evaluator findings): (Glitch A — HIGH, missing_step:final) the step-ledger-stamp.cjs PostToolUse:Agent path was already correct on the standard Agent return shape — F1 in tests/regression/v8.16.0/ now PINS that contract (final-validator return → `final` stamped; raw V1 fence string accepted; errored/ungoverned returns rejected), so a future regression to the stamping path is caught immediately. (Glitch B — MEDIUM, BRAINSTORM_EVIDENCE_MISSING) the v7.14.0 STEP_1_7_ROUTING enforcement requires every MEDIA/COMPLEXA/Spec run to log one of {load-existing, dispatch-brainstorm, no-prep-override, simples-bypass}, but the direct-entry /pipeline path never auto-emitted `no-prep-override` so the final-validator hygiene check raised the violation — now `step-ledger-stamp.cjs` auto-emits a single STEP_1_7_ROUTING(no-prep-override) entry the first time a governed step is stamped on a direct-entry run, idempotent + scoped to qualifying complexity/type and skipped when the run already carries a step_1_7 block or a STEP_1_7_ROUTING gate entry. New agent `agents/quality/diff-discipline-reviewer.md` (tools=Read,Grep,Glob only; static-only inspection; verdict PASS/NEEDS_REDUCTION/REJECTED; max_fix_attempts=5 independent from ADVERSARIAL_BLOCK max=3). review-orchestrator wires diff-discipline as third parallel reviewer alongside adversarial-batch + architecture-reviewer with dual fix_loop_counters. architecture-reviewer adds 5 new checks (abstraction without second use case, new file where local change suffices, unnecessary architecture layer, unrelated refactor, public contract drift) plus 2 reused existing checks (duplicated helper/type/constant, semantic duplication). 7 regression tests in tests/regression/v6.3.0/ (F8-F14). 22-row Mandatory Gates by Complexity invariant preserved (NO new gate added to that table; F1 untouched); Registry stays at 27 rows (F14 defense-in-depth confirms). Bootstrap exception: this release itself created the very enforcement machinery — T1-T3 of v6.3.0 ran without SCOPE LOCK CHECK enforcement (mechanism being born); from T4 onward in this release and all subsequent plans, enforcement is active. Bootstrap flag is one-time-only and locks itself in v6.4.0+. v6.2.0 (2026-05-18) — MINOR. Pre-execution clarification overhaul: replaces fixed 7-question brainstorm template with a 4-phase dynamic agent (Phase A reads project context + likely-affected files BEFORE any question; Phase B inventories gaps across 11 lenses + domain extensions for auth/payment/PII/migration/concurrency; Phase C asks one GATE_REQUEST per real gap with mandatory file:line evidence in option descriptions; Phase D records 02-explore.md + emits structured JSONL telemetry). Adds new step-01b-alternatives agent (proposes 2-4 alternative approaches across Minimal/Pattern-aligned/Aggressive/Contrarian axes after clarification, auto-skips for mechanical tasks). Drift fix on references/complexity-matrix.md: 'Has spec' column for MEDIA changed from 'Optional' to 'Required (auto-dispatch)', matching pipeline-controller.md:281 runtime behavior. Adds 5 new gates (CLARIFICATION_GAPS_DETECTED, CLARIFICATION_RESOLVED, CLARIFICATION_SKIPPED, ALTERNATIVES_PROPOSED, ALTERNATIVE_CHOSEN) plus new AUDIT hardness level (informational telemetry, never blocks, excluded from fidelity scoring). Gate registry 22 → 27. Brainstorm controller step table grows 9 → 10. Dogfood evidence preserved in docs/audits/2026-05-18-clarification-overhaul-dogfood/ (real MEDIA scenario against IFRS 16 contracts.py:232 list_contracts; 14 telemetry events; 4 anti-invention blockers surfaced — streaming/PII/cap/timeout — that the legacy 7-Q template would have left for downstream invention). Backward compat preserved (legacy pipeline-runs/ resume cleanly via missing-field handling on notes.options.alternatives_done). v6.1.0 (2026-05-15) — MINOR. ATDD/BDD/DDD workflow contracts: D5 ATDD non-spec Given/When/Then in quality-gate-router Step 1b, D6 BDD Gherkin per slice in feature-vertical-slice-planner Step 3b, D7 DDD lightweight Bounded Contexts in plan-architect with SOFT BOUNDED_CONTEXT_MISSING event. v6.0.0 (2026-05-15) — MAJOR RELEASE. Comprehensive audit + hardening: 12/12 CRITICAL findings resolved, 22/22 registry gates exercised in runtime, 16 subagents declare Achado #7 protocol explicitly, 3 new security mitigations (HMAC sentinel-state signature, JSONL sanitization parser, pipeline.local.md sandbox parser), 39 regression tests, audit reports archived in docs/audits/2026-05-15-ifrs16-deep-audit/. Effective audit coverage 30% → 95%. Visual documentation overhaul: HTML/SVG flowcharts in docs/diagrams/. Breaking changes: STALE_THRESHOLD_MS constants renamed to canonical STALE_SPAWN_THRESHOLD_MS / STALE_HEARTBEAT_THRESHOLD_MS (aliases preserved until v6.1.0). Migration: hooks consumers should switch to new names; no behavioral change. v5.2.0 (2026-05-08, promoted from v5.2.0-rc.2 with same content; user authorized direct promotion for distribution to all marketplace consumers). v5.2.0-rc.2 (2026-05-08) — Achado #7 protocol adoption expanded to 5 SKILLs (audit/bugfix/feature/spec/pipeline) + brainstorm-controller step agents (step-00-intake, step-01-explore via DISPATCH_REQUEST) + 9 pipeline-controller dispatch sites (Phase 0a/0b/0c, 1.5, 2c/2e, 3a/3b/3c + sentinel) with concrete YAML examples. Closes 3 protocol-coverage lacunas surfaced post-rc.1 adversarial verification. +4 contract regression tests; suite 294 PASS / 10 FAIL (pre-existing Windows CRLF) / 1 SKIPPED. Zero regressions. v5.2.0-rc.1 (2026-05-07) — Achados 1-7 reconciliation + GATE_REQUEST/DISPATCH_REQUEST protocol. Closes 7 contract drift findings from a v5.1.0 dogfood adversarial review. Achados 1-6 are documentation reconciliation (Phase 1.5 plan-mode trigger across 5 surfaces, brainstorm flag propagation, STEP 1.7 classification guard, type=Spec coverage). Achado 7 is architectural: empirical probe confirmed Claude Code harness strips AskUserQuestion/Agent/EnterPlanMode from subagent tool manifests at runtime, regardless of frontmatter; mitigation via emit-and-hoist protocol (3 new block schemas in references/gate-request-protocol.md), adopted in pipeline-controller, brainstorm-controller, executor-controller. Protocol entries live in protocol-events.jsonl (separate from gate-decisions.jsonl) to avoid final-validator's strict 22-gate-registry validation; named gates dual-write for cross-traceability. +13 contract regression tests (suite stays GREEN, zero regressions). 5 follow-ups deferred to v5.2 final: state persistence, lock cleanup, runtime tests, dispatch_id retry, exec-window protocol migration. v5.0.0 — first major release, promoted from v5.0.0-rc.1 with same content. All 7 v5.0 DoD criteria DONE; see CHANGELOG.md [5.0.0] for the promotion summary and [4.17.0] for the technical Wave 8-spec content. v4.17.0 (Wave 8-spec — TRACE.md schema + writer + standalone validator + plan-mode auto-trigger + --no-plan bypass): closes 3 v5.0 DoD criteria in one wave (#2 TRACE.md by default, #3 attachable to PR, #6 plan-mode-enforcer auto-triggers on MEDIA/COMPLEXA). Adds `references/trace-schema/v1.md` (schema_version=1, canonical field order, 1 happy-path + 1 corrupt fixture), `scripts/validate-trace.cjs` (pure-Node validator, exit 0/1 with stderr diff), `tests/integration/trace-writer.test.js` (7 contract tests), `tests/unit/validate-trace.test.js` (5 tests), `tests/unit/plan-mode-trigger.test.js` (5 tests covering MEDIA/COMPLEXA × with/without `--no-plan`). Modifies `agents/core/pipeline-controller.md` Phase 3 closure with new Step 3b-post (TRACE.md emitter wired between final-validator and finishing-branch; opens edit-guard exec-window for cross-tree write to `.pipeline-orchestrator/runs/<run-id>/`); modifies `commands/pipeline.md` Phase 1.5 trigger from `complexity == COMPLEXA` to `complexity ∈ {MEDIA, COMPLEXA} AND --no-plan ausente` plus new `--no-plan` row in modes table; adds asymmetric override semantics (MEDIA + --no-plan = skip+log; COMPLEXA + --no-plan = plan runs anyway, override logged). Corrects `designs/pipeline-orchestrator-v5-consolidated.md` §8 prose to remove ambiguity flagged in `docs/v5-readiness-report.md` §1. Iron Law respected: ZERO changes to gate registry (still 22 in `references/gates.md`), sentinel checkpoints, or agent dispatch namespace; default behavior (no `--no-plan`) preserves v4.16.0 EXACTLY for SIMPLES and COMPLEXA; MEDIA gets plan-architect for the FIRST TIME (additive behavior change, documented in CHANGELOG Behavior Changes section). Suite stays GREEN: 149+17 NEW unit + 7 NEW integration + 5/1 compat + hooks syntax. Wave 8-spec self-hosted dogfood revealed sentinel-hook cwd-discovery brittleness (logged as v5.0-rc.1 follow-up). v4.16.0 (Wave 8-pre / Issue #11 — Real-mode Compat Runner): closes Issue #11 by reimplementing `tests/compat/runner.cjs::executeReal()` from stub to working real-mode CI harness using the `PreToolUse` hook contract that became available in Claude Code v2.1.85+ (`permissionDecision: "allow"` + `updatedInput.answers` to satisfy `AskUserQuestion` in headless mode). Adds `tests/compat/auto-answer-hook.cjs` (generic first-option-default hook with header-keyed overrides via `tests/compat/v4-baseline/answers-overrides.json`), `tests/unit/compat-runner-real.test.js` (8 hook unit tests + 1 conditional real-mode test that skips when claude CLI absent), and splits `.github/workflows/compat-regression.yml` into mock job (always, gate of PR) + real job (opt-in via `vars.CLAUDE_CLI_AVAILABLE=true` + `secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, runs bugfix-fixture smoke on PR or `--all` on `workflow_dispatch`). Updates `designs/slice-0/SPIKE-CI-HARNESS.md` from PARTIAL PASS to PASS. Iron Law respected: ZERO changes to `agents/`, `skills/`, `references/`, `commands/`, `hooks/` (plugin core untouched). Suite stays GREEN: 129 unit + 8 NEW unit + 12 integration + 5 PASS / 1 SKIPPED compat + 8 hooks syntax (exit 0). DoD criterion #5 advances PARTIAL → PARTIAL+. Wave 8 (TRACE.md + plan-mode auto-trigger) remains the next gate to v5.0.0-rc.1. v4.15.0 (Wave 7-spec — v4-to-v5 Migration Doc + v5 Readiness Report): docs-only release that consolidates the v4.0 → v4.14 lineage into `docs/MIGRATION-v4-to-v5.md` (10 sections: lineage table, 13 accumulated breaking changes, FQDN mapping for the v4.9.0 namespace correction, DoD v5.0 status with 3 DONE / 2 PARTIAL / 2 OPEN, compat matrix for FX Studio + Pulsar + custom forks, what v5.0 will/won't change) and adds `docs/v5-readiness-report.md` (1–2-page snapshot at v4.15.0 with the 7-criterion checklist + verification methods + gap analysis + recommended path: Issue #11 → Wave 8 (TRACE.md + plan-mode auto-trigger) → tag v5.0.0-rc.1). Iron Law respected: ZERO changes to agents/, skills/, references/, commands/, hooks/, or tests/. Suite stays GREEN at v4.14.0 levels: 129 unit + 12 integration + 5 PASS / 1 SKIPPED compat + 8 hooks syntax (exit 0). v4.14.0 (Wave 6-spec — Integration Tests + Compat Fixture + Bad-Variant Fixtures): pure additive release that hardens the Wave 1–5 spec lifecycle stack with three layers of net. Adds `tests/integration/spec-workflow-e2e.test.js` (8 new scenarios) covering the spec-format-gate → spec-content-reviewer → spec-post-impl-validator → spec-closer chain, gate hardness/recovery assertions against `references/gates.md` (22-gate registry), `mode_used` mapping in `agents/executor/spec-closer.md` (spec-heavy → full / spec-light → slim / spec-audit-only → audit), and ADVERSARIAL_LOOP_CHECKPOINT counter-reset semantics from v4.13.0 (`shouldEscalate(attempt, batch_changed)` pure function). Adds TEMPLATE compat fixture `tests/compat/v4-baseline/scenarios/spec-fixture/` (input.md + expected.yaml; no mock-output.json) staking out the spec-heavy observable contract for a future dogfooding session — runner.cjs auto-discovers and SKIPs it via the case-insensitive TEMPLATE check. Adds three bad-variant spec fixtures under `tests/fixtures/specs/`: `auth-flow-bad-yaml/` (intentionally unparseable spec.json → SPEC_FORMAT_GATE_FAIL HARD via critical-artifact rule), `auth-flow-no-traceability/` (design.md stripped of Traces-to + Glossary + responsibilities + data model + contracts → ≥8 of 25 checks fail → score ≤17 → NO-GO), `auth-flow-missing-artifact/` (tasks.md absent → SPEC_ARTIFACT_MISSING MANDATORY before format scoring runs). One surgical edit in `tests/unit/compat-baselines.test.js` relaxes a hard-coded SKIPPED regex (0 → 0 or 1) to accommodate the new TEMPLATE fixture, with comment pointing to the future tighten-back trigger. Iron Law respected: ZERO changes to agents/, skills/, references/, commands/, hooks/. Suite: 129 unit + 12 integration (was 4) + 5 PASS / 1 SKIPPED compat, all exit 0. v4.13.0 (Gates Invariants & Mode Persistence — Wave 5-spec): adds 6 spec gates to references/gates.md registry (SSOT sync with pipeline-controller.md Inline Invariants, closing ARCH-1 gap from v4.11.0 adversarial review); extends commands/pipeline.md Inline Invariants from 15 to 21 gate names; adds Spec row to complexity-matrix.md Pipeline Routing Matrix; introduces mode_used field (slim|full|audit) to spec-context-schema.md as spec.json field; wires mode_used write step into spec-closer.md based on pipeline_variant. +16 unit tests (gates registry parsing, escalation rule, variant→mode mapping, regression baseline). v4.12.0 (Wave 4-spec — /spec entry-point + 3 pipeline composition refs): adds the thin manual-only `skills/spec/SKILL.md` entry-point (mirrors skills/bugfix, skills/audit, skills/feature pattern), pre-classifying `task_type=Spec` to skip the type-detection round in task-orchestrator. Variant flags `--light` / `--heavy` / `--audit-only` route directly to skills/spec-light, spec-heavy, or spec-audit-only with their prescriptive 6/9/5-step procedures (Wave 2). Without a flag, dispatches to pipeline-controller, which uses the Wave 3-spec 4-signal classifier to select the right variant. Three new pipeline composition reflexes (`references/pipelines/spec-light.md`, `spec-heavy.md`, `spec-audit-only.md`) document team composition + step-by-step flow with 1:1 fidelity to their SKILL.md sequence_lock — enforced by a new cross-ref test (`tests/unit/spec-entry-point-and-pipelines.test.js`, +8 tests, suite 103→111). Backward compat preserved: `/pipeline-orchestrator:pipeline` full classifier path is unchanged; existing entry-points (bugfix/audit/feature) untouched. Zero changes to agents/, no changes to skills/spec-light, skills/spec-heavy, skills/spec-audit-only, references/gates.md, or commands/pipeline.md (purely additive). v4.11.0 (Wave 3-spec — Spec Classifier Routing): wires type=Spec first-class classification into task-orchestrator (4-signal detection: explicit path / --type=spec flag / prose regex / glob fallback), routes spec-light/spec-heavy/spec-audit-only via pipeline-controller skill dispatch (mirrors bugfix pattern), adds 5 spec sentinel checkpoints (guarded on pipeline_variant.startsWith("spec-")), AC-seeded ATDD in quality-gate-router (1 scenario per AC, EARS preserved + bullet normalized), GO/CONDITIONAL/NO-GO vs PASS/FAIL/SKIPPED vocabulary doc in audit-trail.md. Backward compat preserved (non-spec pipelines route exactly as v4.10.0); zero changes to Wave 1 spec lifecycle agents or Wave 2 spec skill trees. v4.10.0 (Wave 3 — Compat Baselines): graduates all 5 compat fixtures (audit/bugfix/feature/hotfix/ux) from TEMPLATE → REAL baselines captured 2026-05-05 against pipeline v4.10.0. Each fixture now ships paired `expected.yaml` + `mock-output.json` with the 5 observable contract fields (classification, gates_triggered, agents_invoked, artifacts_produced, verdict). Real-mode wiring of the runner remains a Slice 4-extended item: `executeReal` is still a stub due to upstream `--ci-auto-confirm` gap in Claude Code (documented in `designs/slice-0/SPIKE-CI-HARNESS.md`). v4.9.x: spec workflow polish + CI debt. v4.9.0 (Spec Workflow Import — Wave 2): imports 3 prescriptive skill trees. v4.8.0 (Hook Enforcement). v4.7.0 (Slice 3b polish). v4.6.x (Slice 3b feature workflow). v4.5.0 (Slice 3a audit). v4.4.x (Slice 1.5 bugfix import). v4.3.x (skill migration). v4.2.x (SEC patches + thin entry-points). v4 base: orchestration in isolated controller agent.

entries.title: dotlight-skillset
entries.description: A lightweight curated Claude Code skillset for .NET developers. Combines a customized fork of obra/superpowers v6 (workflow), a trimmed fork of Aaronontheweb/dotnet-skills (patterns + agents), and adapted skills from mattpocock/skills, hsmejky/skills, and DietrichGebert/ponytail. Tuned for .NET 10 + Aspire + Minimal API + NHibernate or EF Core + Postgres/TimescaleDB projects with Vue or Blazor frontends. Key overrides: AskUserQuestion-first brainstorming, domain-first AND persistence-first planning before TDD, lazy-senior-dev minimalism (reviewability as a constraint), database-design-conventions + database-review quality gate, and executing-plans preferred over subagent-driven-development.
